capabilities.description: Read a Factorio credential from an operating-system-backed credential provider.
Discover candidate Factorio installations without taking ownership or mutating them.
Apply an exact adoption plan without mutating Steam-owned installations.
Plan explicit adoption of an eligible standalone installation.
Inspect an existing portable installation without mutation or ownership transfer.
Apply a reviewed managed-install plan through Universal Setup.
Produce an immutable plan for a new FacMan-managed standalone installation.
Project a registered installation into independent source, deployment, ownership, data, integration, provenance, verification, and filesystem capability axes.
Compare current installation evidence with desired state and produce a deterministic, non-mutating reconciliation plan.
Register a reference to an eligible existing installation while preserving foreign ownership.
Run with no persistent change outside the authorised FacMan workspace.
Run with FacMan-owned instance data isolated while explicitly disclosing enumerated platform-owned state.
Revalidate launch identity, paths, ownership, configuration, and isolation immediately before execution.
Render the exact executable, arguments, configuration, roots, and advertised isolation mode.
Read Factorio Mod Portal metadata through an explicit network capability.
Perform authenticated Mod Portal mutations through an explicit network capability.
Start and supervise an explicitly authorised process without shell invocation under timeout, cancellation, output, environment, and process-tree rules; real Factorio use remains separately gated.
Publish a signed, provenance-bound release through an explicitly authorised channel.
Sign a release artifact with separately authorised publisher credentials.

claim_levels.claim: Managed paths remain inside the workspace
claim_levels.limitation: new filesystem features must reuse these primitives

claim_levels.level: regression-proven for current writers and same-host commit races
claim_levels.limitation: ancestor substitution and power-loss durability remain unproven

claim_levels.claim: Package tools clean only owned output
claim_levels.limitation: other future destructive tools need their own ownership kind
real Factorio write semantics unproven
proves FacMan and probe behavior, not Factorio internals
internal test authority only; no real Factorio guarantee
protected Steam state changed; standalone remains unproven; execution remains blocked
shared/network and multi-host filesystems remain unproven

claim_levels.claim: Reviewed local diagnostic export is bounded
claim_levels.limitation: no arbitrary formats, GUI UX, shared filesystems, or authenticity
remaining legacy commands and goldens are not implicitly promoted
new descriptor versions and handlers require the same parity proof

claim_levels.claim: Run preview uses the authoritative route
claim_levels.limitation: no process starts; real Factorio remains unproven

claim_levels.claim: Windows, Linux, and macOS install discovery is read-only
claim_levels.limitation: scoped roots/layouts only; no package-manager or Spotlight execution

claim_levels.claim: Explicit version-library discovery finds structural numeric children without mutating them
claim_levels.limitation: discovery does not confer setup ownership

claim_levels.claim: Program-local Factorio data can be preserved in an isolated instance
claim_levels.limitation: temp is skipped; no installer identity is created
does not execute Factorio or prove operator usability

claim_levels.claim: Functional TUI packages run on three x64 targets
claim_levels.limitation: unsigned and unpublished; no OS-neutral or ARM64 claim

claim_levels.claim: Windows x64 static CLI package runs after relocation
claim_levels.limitation: local Windows x64 only; unsigned and unpublished

claim_levels.claim: Linux x64 CLI tarball runs after relocation
claim_levels.limitation: Ubuntu 24.04 x64/glibc 2.39 only; rerun per revision; unsigned and unpublished

claim_levels.claim: macOS x64 CLI tarball runs after relocation
claim_levels.limitation: Intel runner and target 13.0 only; no Apple Silicon, AppKit runtime, signing, or publication claim
authenticity and live-target setup acceptance remain unproven
new policy-approved targets only; no real Factorio or execution
not general availability; existing installations and all higher-risk scopes remain excluded
newly created, explicitly selected policy-approved targets only
presentation only; Setup owns policy; broader authority remains excluded
bounded synthetic lane only; Windows/macOS multi-volume and real archives unproven
superseded only for the automated synthetic lane; no human Pass claimed
managed candidate only; no Factorio, existing install, execution, H1, or external authority
synthetic/local managed candidate only; every higher-risk authority remains excluded
no adoption apply, deletion, mutation, Steam adoption, execution, or external authority
menu only; no preparation, permit, credentials, network, or execution
no issuance, Play, Setup, credentials, network, signing, or publication authority
process-tree claim only; no candidate, issuance, execution, verdict, or authority
no Factorio, packet, Play authority, Steam, network, credentials, or Setup
signatures or trusted external metadata required

claim_levels.claim: Package inputs and provenance are recorded
claim_levels.limitation: licensing is proven at pinned revisions; unsigned evidence is not publisher authentication

claim_levels.claim: Experimental FLB ABI and installed SDK have a correctness floor
claim_levels.limitation: stable compatibility, binary replay, and the full compiler matrix remain unproven

claim_levels.claim: CLI uses only the client boundary
claim_levels.limitation: process and daemon transports remain unavailable
other targets need per-revision CI; output is unsigned

claim_levels.claim: Normal AIDE context excludes history bulk
claim_levels.limitation: development governance only; no product or human proof

execution_modes.boundary: FacMan-owned instance data is isolated; enumerated Steam/platform state may change after explicit disclosure.
No persistent change may occur outside the authorised FacMan workspace.

hermetic_standalone_play_verdict.previous_provider_refusal_message: independent observer was not active before process boundary

known_blockers: Installation model v2 and deterministic reconciliation planning are complete read-only; authenticated source inspection and transaction-backed lifecycle apply remain unavailable.

Official Factorio Windows installers share uninstall registration identities; installing an older version can supersede the current 2.1 Add/Remove Programs registration even when program directories are separate.
Steam-aware instance-isolated Play and hermetic standalone Play are independent, unproven real-product gates.
Gate 4C verdict attempt 01 is Inconclusive; the observer-start defect is repaired and live-proven without Factorio.
Gate 4C verdict attempt 02 is blocked before baseline because the elevated harness would create Factorio in its calling security context; privilege separation repair is active.
The current desktop UI exposes commands rather than the first-run instance-to-Factorio-menu journey.
AppKit remains compile-only until an actual bundle runtime invocation is recorded.
Artifacts are unsigned and unpublished; integrity and provenance do not authenticate a publisher.

product.charter: Create any number of independent Factorio setups, select one, and launch the normal game as though it had always been installed and configured exactly that way.
product.golden_journey: find Factorio -> select/create instance -> choose version/preset/profiles/modpack/accounts -> inspect readiness -> prepare if needed -> Play to menu -> start/load/join/edit -> exit -> preserve state -> relaunch
product.platform_scope: Windows-first playable alpha; CLI and TUI remain complete advanced interfaces.
product.primary_persona: A Factorio player who wants multiple complete, isolated game environments without rebuilding versions, mods, profiles, accounts, or settings by hand.

quarantined_capabilities: install.managed.apply outside newly created, explicitly selected, policy-approved managed targets
truth_boundaries: AIDE is development governance only and never a product dependency.
Focused affected tests do not replace the full promotion matrix.
Human acceptance is never inferred from automated checks.
Compile, runtime, package, publication, and support status are separate claims.
Historical milestone evidence does not select the current product objective.
